article_title: Miami Startup Subquadratic Emerges From Stealth With $29M and a 12-Million-Token Model It Says Beats Frontier Compute by 1,000x
summary: Substantively sound stealth-launch piece; two minor recoverable issues (architecture-name word-order error and a paraphrase wrapped in quote marks) covered by a single corrections record on merge.

findings.details: Article calls the company's architecture 'Sparse Subquadratic Attention'. Both sources name it 'Subquadratic Sparse Attention' (SSA). Word order inverted; substance unchanged.

findings.message: Direct quote attributed to VentureBeat does not appear verbatim
findings.details: Article quotes 'selectively focuses only on the token comparisons that matter rather than computing attention across every possible relationship' attributed to VentureBeat. The exact phrasing is not in the snapshot. VentureBeat's actual wording is paraphrastic: 'most of the token-to-token comparisons in standard attention are wasted compute. Instead of comparing every token to every other token, SSA learns to identify which comparisons actually matter and computes attention only over those positions.' Same substance, paraphrased and wrapped in quote marks.

content_preview.title: Miami Startup Subquadratic Emerges From Stealth With $29M and a 12-Million-Token Model It Says Beats Frontier Compute by 1,000x
content_preview.summary: Subquadratic launched SubQ on May 5, 2026 with a 12 million token context window and benchmarks it claims undercut Claude Opus by orders of magnitude. AI researchers are split between fascination and accusations of vaporware.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

A Miami-based startup called Subquadratic emerged from stealth on May 5, 2026 with a $29 million seed round and a large language model it claims breaks the quadratic compute scaling that has defined transformers since 2017. The company's first model, SubQ 1M-Preview, runs on what Subquadratic calls a Sparse Subquadratic Attention architecture and exposes a 12-million-token context window — about ten times the longest windows in production from frontier labs — at a fraction of the co...

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured 799-word News piece with clear separation of company-supplied claims and outside reaction. Strong 'What We Don't Know' section and analysis section that explicitly hedges on benchmark replicability.
editor_notes.source_verification: Both sources fetched cleanly (200 OK). Read both snapshots from disk. $29M seed, Justin Mateen/Tinder, Javier Villamizar/SoftBank Vision Fund, CEO Justin Dangel, CTO Alexander Whedon, 12-million-token context window, RULER 128K benchmark numbers (95% / $8 vs 94% / $2,600 for Claude Opus), Anthropic/OpenAI/Stripe/Brex investor lineup, SubQ Code/SubQ Search products, May 5 2026 launch — all verified verbatim across the two snapshots. Rysana quotes ('subquadratic attention done well which is very meaningful for long context workloads' and 'odds of it being BS are extremely low') verified verbatim in VentureBeat. 'genuine curiosity to open accusations of vaporware' verified verbatim in VentureBeat. Dangel quote 'transition from a dense attention, quadratic scaling architecture to a sparse attention linear architecture' verified verbatim in SiliconANGLE.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Headline, summary, and lead claims fully sourced. Two minor attribution issues flagged below — neither affects the substance.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and explicitly skeptical where appropriate. Distinguishes between company-supplied numbers and independently-verified results. No editorializing.
concerns: Architecture name reversed: article uses 'Sparse Subquadratic Attention'; both sources call it 'Subquadratic Sparse Attention' (SSA). Word-order error, substance unchanged.

Direct quote 'selectively focuses only on the token comparisons that matter rather than computing attention across every possible relationship' attributed to VentureBeat is paraphrased, not verbatim. The actual VentureBeat wording is different but substantively equivalent.
recommendations: Future submissions: never wrap paraphrased text in quote marks; either reproduce verbatim or use indirect speech.
Cross-check proper-noun word order (architecture names, product names) against the canonical source before submission.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Substantively sound article with strong skeptical framing. Two minor attribution slips (one inverted name, one paraphrase-in-quotes) are exactly the kind of recoverable issues APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS handles: a single corrections note can honestly inform readers.
